body_txt: A specific type of Ethnic Magician , centered around indigenous peoples (for example, Native Americans, Canadian First Nations, and Aboriginal Australians) and fantasy races based around them . Natives that fall under this trope have magical powers coming from innate spirituality or closeness to nature that "civilized" races don't have. Such powers usually involve influence over nature or animals, or other spirit powers. Quite often, the Native in question will be dressed very "traditionally" even in modern settings. May sometimes speak-um Tonto Talk . Overlap with Badass Native is far from uncommon. If the indigenous magic comes from beyond the grave, see Indian Burial Ground . Works often use this trope to promote a "positive" image of indigenous peoples and especially for Green Aesop rather than accurately portraying their culture or developing them as characters. Like Noble Savage , this trope can have obvious Unfortunate Implications . While this trope does render indigenous peoples badass in their own right , it usually furthers stereotypes of them as exotic outsiders, and often trivialises deep spiritual traditions as mere fantastical magic in a manner that many real indigenous people view as disrespectful (compare how Hollywood Voodoo treats the similarly real religion Voudoun ). It’s especially bad with Green Aesop , usually crossing a line from mere awe and respect for nature to stereotypical oversimplified nature worship, even if they don’t have actual powers. In addition to stereotypes and appropriation, it often kneecaps itself, as it mystifies the very objective and pragmatic science of ecology. Not to mention many indigenous nature centric traditions are more rooted in how they have survived and lived off the land for centuries rather than protecting nature for nature’s sake. In some cases the characters are often framed as luddite and anti-technology in favour of being one with nature, ignoring the vast empires and complex technologies Indigenous Americans had (though tech-savvy examples do exist). Many are especially tech savvy today due to how the United States is considered one of the tech capitals of the world. Because Tropes Are Tools , this can be subverted to explore how technology and nature should be symbolic and balanced rather than one being rejected. Of course, it all does depend on the work the character is in. A work where a lot of notable characters are magic users from across the world is where you would expect to find an indigenous magic user, after all. If this character is a superhero, see Captain Ethnic . See also Magical Negro and Magical Asian . Contrast with Hollywood Natives . noreallife
